title: STAR Identities: Three Arithmetic Equalities Whose Sole Solution in [1, N] is n=6

foundation_axiom.statement: sigma(n) * phi(n) = n * tau(n) = 24 iff n = 6 (n >= 2)
foundation_axiom.absoluteness: Pi_0_1 arithmetical -> Delta_0 absolute -> ZFC / large-cardinal / Reinhardt / Cantor Absolute 𝔚 invariant

n8128_evaluation.note: post-hoc verification, not in original sweep

proof_technique_candidates: even-perfect direct (Euclid-Euler factorization) — DONE in paper §3.1
multiplicative-additive hybrid (sigma, tau multiplicative; Omega strongly additive) — open
identities.n6_unique_evidence: for n in {2, 3, 4, 5, 7, 8, 9, 10, 11, 12, ..., 2000} \ {6, 28, 496}, identity is false (verified by exhaustive sweep)
predicts: Omega-variant is a necessary-and-sufficient characterization of perfect numbers

predicts_status.perfect_iff_STAR1: OPEN — even direction sketched, odd-perfect case vacuous, non-perfect counterexample search bounded by N=8128

identities.verification_scope: computer-checked for n in [1, 2000] + perfect numbers up to 8128; no general proof

n6_evaluation.note: ω(6) = 2 = φ(6) at n=6, so STAR-2 collapses to MASTER (σφ=nτ) at n=6

identities.n6_unique_evidence: for n in [1, 300] \ {6}, identity is false; sweep recorded in search_new_identities.hexa B8
proof_technique_candidates: ω(6) = φ(6) = 2 coincidence at n=6 — explains LHS match
general n: ω(n) ≠ φ(n) (e.g., n=4: ω=1, φ=2), so MASTER and STAR-2 diverge
predicts: n <= 10000 extension: n=6 still unique solution
ω-variant is the 17th candidate for Bernoulli independence
identities.verification_scope: computer-checked for n in [1, 300] only; range extension required for stronger claim

identities.statement_unicode: σ(n) · φ(n) · ω(n) = n · τ(n) · Ω(n)
identities.statement_ascii: sigma(n) * phi(n) * omega_small(n) = n * tau(n) * Omega_big(n)
identities.n6_unique_evidence: for n in [1, 2000] \ {6}, identity is false; sweep recorded in omega_identity_search.hexa item 4c
proof_technique_candidates: 4-factor identity — probabilistic argument: random multiplicative match has probability ~ 1/n^2 scale, single solution in [1,2000] is highly significant signal
Π_0_1 absoluteness lift to Δ_0-absolute — possible if proven for all n
identities.verification_scope: computer-checked for n in [1, 2000]; no general proof

comparison_with_master.master_statement: σ(n) · φ(n) = n · τ(n) iff n = 6 (n >= 2)
comparison_with_master.master_proven_in: computer-checked n in [2, 10^4] + 3 independent proofs (n6-architecture SIG-ATLAS-203)

star_vs_master.common: all four are σ·f(n) = n·g(n) family with f, g multiplicative or additive
star_vs_master.STAR_1_vs_MASTER: STAR-1 admits {6, 28, 496, ...} (perfect numbers) while MASTER admits {6} only
star_vs_master.STAR_2_vs_MASTER: STAR-2 collapses to MASTER at n=6 due to ω(6)=φ(6)=2; differs elsewhere
star_vs_master.STAR_3_vs_MASTER: STAR-3 is a 4-factor extension; reduces neither to MASTER nor STAR-2

open_problems.statement: Prove or disprove: STAR-1 (σΩ=nτ) admits exactly the perfect numbers as solutions, for all n.
open_problems.current_status: even-perfect direction sketched (paper §3.1); reverse direction (STAR-1 ⟹ perfect) open; odd-perfect case vacuous since no odd perfect known.
open_problems.difficulty_estimate: medium — multiplicative-additive hybrid; Sylvester-style minimal counterexample may work.

open_problems.statement: Extend STAR-2 sweep to N = 10^4. Is n=6 still the unique solution?
open_problems.current_status: open (predicts placeholder; sweep cost ~1 minute on hexa stage1).

open_problems.statement: Are STAR-1, STAR-2, STAR-3 all Δ_0-absolute (lift to [11*])? Each is Π_0_1 if proven for all n.
open_problems.current_status: Π_0_1 form confirmed (all functions primitive recursive); Δ_0-absoluteness conditional on general proofs.
open_problems.difficulty_estimate: high — depends on OP-1 and analogous proofs.

open_problems.statement: Find a unifying algebraic condition that subsumes MASTER + STAR-1/2/3 + K2/K3 + B-family as a single identity family.
open_problems.current_status: open; family enumerated in paper §3.4 but no closed-form characterization.
open_problems.difficulty_estimate: high — requires unifying multiplicative + additive function theory.

next_star_candidates.n6_check: σ(6)·J_2(6) = 12·24 = 288, n²·τ(6) = 36·4 = 144 — FAIL at n=6, requires reformulation

next_star_candidates.statement_proposed: ψ · φ · ω = n · τ · Ω (STAR-3 with σ→ψ)
next_star_candidates.n6_check: ψ(6)=12 (Dedekind), 12·2·2 = 48, 6·4·2 = 48 — HOLDS at n=6, sweep needed
M(6)=7, 7·12 = 84, 2·16 = 32 — FAIL at n=6, requires coefficient adjustment
sopfr(6)=5, 12·5 = 60, 6·6 = 36 — FAIL at n=6, requires factor adjustment

ultra_uniformity.statement: σφ=nτ unique n=6 is invariant under Knuth ↑↑↑↑, ordinal ω/ε_0/Ω, Busy Beaver, TREE, Rayo, large cardinal, Reinhardt, Berkeley, Cantor 𝔚
ultra_uniformity.star_inheritance_status: candidate — all three STAR identities are Π_0_1 (since σ, τ, φ, ω, Ω are primitive recursive), but Δ_0-absolute promotion requires general proof for all n (cf. OP-1, OP-3)

cross_repo_signals: SIG-ATLAS-203 (n6-architecture): 3 independent proofs of MASTER + atlas-based
